evals.prompt: A skill's SKILL.md description was edited. Run /z-eval-update --apply and accept every proposed change.
evals.expected_output: Only generated cases for that skill are regenerated; the source_hash and last_updated are refreshed; user-authored cases in the same evals.json are untouched; manifest.history.yml has one new appended snapshot.
assertions: Only cases with _meta.generated === true on the affected target are modified
User-authored cases (no _meta, or _meta.generated === false) are byte-for-byte identical after the apply
Every regenerated case has a fresh _meta.source_hash matching the current target content_hash
Every regenerated case has a fresh _meta.last_updated in ISO-8601 format
manifest.yml updated_at is newer than its created_at
manifest.history.yml has exactly one new snapshot appended
Every accept/reject choice was presented via askQuestion

evals.prompt: Run /z-eval-update --check on a repo where a covered target has been deleted.
evals.expected_output: Exit code 2 (critical drift), stderr contains a JSON-line delta with kind=removed and critical=true. No file writes.
assertions: Process exits with code 2
Stdout includes a 'removed' count in the summary line
Stderr includes at least one JSON-line delta with "kind":"removed" and "critical":true
No files were written (manifest.yml and evals.json files are unchanged)

evals.prompt: Edit discoveryTargets in config.json to add 'lib', then run /z-eval-update --check.
evals.expected_output: Exit code 0. The check does not report drift attributable to the config edit — rediscovery uses manifest.discovery_config.
assertions: Process exits with code 0
The summary does not attribute drift to the config change
manifest.discovery_config is unchanged after the check

evals.prompt: We finished editing a SKILL.md under the configured skills root and want a sanity pass before approving anything. Following zoto-update-evals, walk me through what `/z-eval-update` with no extra flags loads first, whether it writes anywhere, which discovery snapshot full-catalog rediscovery must honor, and why per-target analyser stamp paths do not re-apply ignore globs from discover.
assertions: Step 1 loads `.zoto/eval-system/config.yml` and `.zoto/eval-system/manifest.yml` before calling `readManifestSnapshot()`.
The no-argument invocation is a rediscovery dry run that does not write regenerated eval artifacts.
Full-catalog rediscovery honors `manifest.discovery_config` for skillsRoots, discoveryTargets, and ignore globs rather than live `config.yml` discovery fields.
Per-target analyser and stamper paths do not re-apply discover ignore filters unless the operator reapplies them out of band.
Active static framework selection comes from `readManifestSnapshot()` via the `static.framework` field.
Immediately after `/z-eval-create`, a bare `/z-eval-update` dry run should surface no unexpected critical drift when every discovered hash still matches the manifest snapshot.

primitive_analysis.summary: The skill governs diff-aware eval refresh across check, dry-run, targeted, and apply modes with manifest-snapshot rediscovery, per-primitive analyser refresh or CI cache reuse, framework-specific static stamping plus unified LLM regeneration, and strict preservation of user-authored cases and test files.

evals.expected_output: The reply lists config and manifest reads plus snapshot access, confirms the bare invocation is a rediscovery dry run with no regenerated eval writes, cites manifest.discovery_config for skillsRoots and discoveryTargets, notes ignore globs apply during discover and snapshot capture but not during eval:analyse or eval-stamp unless replicated manually, and states a freshly bootstrapped tree should classify targets unchanged when hashes still match.

evals.prompt: Onboarding checklist: `.zoto/eval-system/config.yml` exists but `.zoto/eval-system/manifest.yml` is absent. According to zoto-update-evals, what single abort message must the updater surface and what prior command should operators run instead?
assertions: When `.zoto/eval-system/manifest.yml` is missing, the updater aborts with the documented string `Run /z-eval-create first.`

primitive_analysis.summary: The skill governs diff-aware eval refresh across check, dry-run, targeted, and apply modes with manifest-snapshot rediscovery, per-primitive analyser refresh or CI cache reuse, framework-specific static stamping plus unified LLM regeneration, and strict preservation of user-authored cases and test files.

evals.expected_output: The assistant states work stops until the manifest exists and quotes the documented prerequisite instruction exactly.

evals.prompt: We are wiring `/z-eval-update --check` into a headless CI job. Outline every prerequisite script it invokes before delta review, whether it may prompt, how critical drift reaches stderr, and which exit statuses mean parity plus drift cleared versus gated failure defaults.
assertions: Check mode executes `pnpm exec tsx scripts/check-analyser-payload-parity.ts` before interpreting drift so TS and Python analyser payloads stay aligned.
The mode stays non-interactive with no prompts for operators or the skill implementation.
Stdout prints a JSON summary line and stderr emits per-critical-delta JSON lines when problems exist.
Exit status 0 means clean parity and no blocking drift, while unresolved critical deltas return `config.update.checkExitCodeOnCriticalDrift` defaulting to 2.
Parity violations surface under the `parity_drift` key inside the emitted report.

primitive_analysis.summary: The skill governs diff-aware eval refresh across check, dry-run, targeted, and apply modes with manifest-snapshot rediscovery, per-primitive analyser refresh or CI cache reuse, framework-specific static stamping plus unified LLM regeneration, and strict preservation of user-authored cases and test files.

evals.expected_output: The reply cites the analyser payload parity harness, stresses fully non-interactive behaviour with no operator prompts, mentions JSON summary on stdout plus per-critical-delta JSON lines on stderr, and names exit 0 versus the configurable critical-drift exit defaulting to 2.

evals.prompt: A covered primitive's content hash changed upstream. Explain the default `--apply` sequence after classification: where `runAnalyser({ target, invalidate: true })` fires, how pytest versus vitest versus jest static stamping is chosen, how LLM eval JSON is re-stamped, how manifest bookkeeping updates, what JSON artefact summarizes the job, and who owns operator confirmations relative to the skill body.
assertions: Added or modified targets call `pnpm run eval:analyse` through `runAnalyser` unless `--no-analyser` or CI without `--with-analyser` loads cached payloads instead.
`static.framework` routes to `regeneratePytest()`, `regenerateVitest()`, or `regenerateJest()` and their per-primitive stampers.
Every drifted target invokes `regenerateLlm()` to re-stamp the co-located `<kind>/evals/<name>.json` with no separate LLM strategy axis.
Successful writes refresh `.zoto/eval-system/manifest.yml` with renewed `git_ref`, `updated_at`, `generated_by: zoto-update-evals`, and rediscovered `targets` carrying fresh `content_hash` values.
History handling appends a new snapshot to `.zoto/eval-system/manifest.history.yml` without compacting prior entries.
The terminal summary object includes `mode`, `regenerated_targets`, `files_written`, `files_preserved_user_authored`, `user_cases_preserved`, and `reports[]` with per-framework metrics.
Interactive confirmations are owned by the `/z-eval-update` command resume path, not emitted from the skill implementation itself.

primitive_analysis.summary: The skill governs diff-aware eval refresh across check, dry-run, targeted, and apply modes with manifest-snapshot rediscovery, per-primitive analyser refresh or CI cache reuse, framework-specific static stamping plus unified LLM regeneration, and strict preservation of user-authored cases and test files.

evals.expected_output: The narration walks added or modified targets through fresh analyser refresh, static framework dispatch, unified LLM regeneration, refreshed manifest fields, an appended history snapshot, a structured closing summary, and defers interactive confirmations to the command resume path rather than the skill.

evals.prompt: Operators need a narrow refresh: `/z-eval-update --target 'skill:zoto-*' --apply`. Map how the glob matches discovered metadata, which discovery configuration applies, how deltas are scoped, and where approval prompts live relative to the skill body.
assertions: Glob resolution compares the pattern against either `target.path` or `target.id` using dot-aware rules from the discovered inventory.
Targeted `--target` invocations enumerate discovery from live `.zoto/eval-system/config.yml` rather than the stored manifest snapshot alone.
Only the filtered subset of discovered targets participates in delta classification and stamping.
Apply-mode confirmations remain command-owned resumes rather than skill-level prompts.

primitive_analysis.summary: The skill governs diff-aware eval refresh across check, dry-run, targeted, and apply modes with manifest-snapshot rediscovery, per-primitive analyser refresh or CI cache reuse, framework-specific static stamping plus unified LLM regeneration, and strict preservation of user-authored cases and test files.

evals.expected_output: The answer ties glob resolution to path or id fields, notes targeted runs use live config.yml discovery fields, limits delta computation to the matched subset, and reminds that apply decisions flow through the command resume channel.

evals.prompt: `CI=true` with `--no-analyser --apply` is our default pipeline speed path. Explain where cached analyser payloads load from on disk, the stderr banner CI hosts emit, how `--with-analyser` opts back into LLM refresh, and when `update.failOnNoAnalyserInCI` escalates failures.
assertions: Bypassing LLM refreshes loads analyser artefacts from `.zoto/eval-system/cache/analyser/<source_hash>.json` keyed by each primitive SHA-256 source hash digest.
When `process.env.CI === "true"` without `--with-analyser`, stderr logs `[CI WARNING] skipping fresh primitive analysis in CI; reusing payloads from .zoto/eval-system/cache/analyser/`.
Combining `--with-analyser` with CI forces fresh `runAnalyser` calls despite `CI=true`.
Setting `update.failOnNoAnalyserInCI: true` terminates the updater with exit code 5 under CI cached-analysis runs.

primitive_analysis.summary: The skill governs diff-aware eval refresh across check, dry-run, targeted, and apply modes with manifest-snapshot rediscovery, per-primitive analyser refresh or CI cache reuse, framework-specific static stamping plus unified LLM regeneration, and strict preservation of user-authored cases and test files.

evals.expected_output: The guidance names the hashed cache directory under `.zoto/eval-system/cache/analyser/`, cites the CI stderr warning about skipping fresh primitive analysis, documents `--with-analyser` forcing fresh runs even on CI, and notes exit 5 when fail-on-no-analyser escalation is configured.

evals.prompt: Teach QA how zoto-update-evals buckets manifest deltas and maps each modification subtype to its criticality knobs, including whitespace-only churn.
assertions: Classification recognizes `added`, `removed`, `modified`, and `unchanged` targets between manifest snapshots and freshly discovered inventories.
Criticality for additions depends on `addedTargetWithoutCoverage` when enabled.
Removed targets hinge on coverage by active generated cases gated by `removedTargetWithActiveCases`.
Frontmatter name or description deltas consult `skillFrontmatterChange`, while behavioural surface deltas consult `publicSurfaceChange`.
Comment-only or whitespace churn is explicitly non-critical per the surgical-diff policy.

primitive_analysis.summary: The skill governs diff-aware eval refresh across check, dry-run, targeted, and apply modes with manifest-snapshot rediscovery, per-primitive analyser refresh or CI cache reuse, framework-specific static stamping plus unified LLM regeneration, and strict preservation of user-authored cases and test files.

evals.expected_output: The rundown enumerates added, removed, modified, and unchanged classifications, aligns each with matching criticalChangeRules toggles, and marks comment or whitespace deltas as non-critical.

evals.prompt: We ship mixed `evals.json` bundles and handwritten Vitest runner files. Summarize every runtime and compile guard in `plugins/zoto-eval-system/engine/_user-case-guards.ts`, marking rules for `_meta.generated` on cases versus generated file markers on test files, and note how helpers record skipped paths versus surgical JSON replacement.
assertions: `isGeneratedCase` leaves rows without `_meta` or with `_meta.generated === false` byte-identical while replacing only stamped generated rows via `surgicallyReplaceGeneratedCases()`.
`isGeneratedFile` refuses overwriting user-authored `*.test.ts`, `*.test.js`, or `*.test.py` runner and scenario files, logging `manual_merge_required` and listing skips under `files_preserved`.
JSON LLM eval helpers require a top-level `_meta.generated` envelope before overwriting a generated `*.json` eval file lacking user authorship markers.
Mixed `evals.json` updates route through `json-source-map` pointer splicing rather than rewriting entire documents when only generated sections change.

Unit expectations in `scripts/__tests__/eval-update-guards.test.ts` assert user rows stay untouched, unmarked generated tests survive, marked generated tests regenerate, and CI cached analyser stderr matches the guarded warning substring.

primitive_analysis.summary: The skill governs diff-aware eval refresh across check, dry-run, targeted, and apply modes with manifest-snapshot rediscovery, per-primitive analyser refresh or CI cache reuse, framework-specific static stamping plus unified LLM regeneration, and strict preservation of user-authored cases and test files.

evals.expected_output: The explanation distinguishes `isGeneratedCase` protections for mixed JSON arrays from `isGeneratedFile` skips, cites manual_merge_required notes for unmarked generated tests, and reinforces surgical JSON patching rather than whole-file rewrites.

evals.prompt: Document every filesystem location zoto-update-evals examines for drift, including mirrored plugin and `.cursor` command or agent manifests plus the canonical hook identifier noted in the skill.
assertions: Skills read coverage from nested `evals/evals.json` files inside each declared skill directory rooted at configured `skillsRoots` paths.
Commands reconcile plugin-scoped JSON under `plugins/<plugin>/evals/commands/` alongside sibling copies under `.cursor/evals/commands/`.
Agents reconcile plugin-scoped JSON under `plugins/<plugin>/evals/agents/` alongside sibling copies under `.cursor/evals/agents/`.

Hooks reconcile plugin-level hook manifests under `plugins/<plugin>/evals/hooks/` together with `.cursor/evals/hooks/hooks.json`, treating `hook:cursor-workspace` as the canonical bundled hook target identifier.

primitive_analysis.summary: The skill governs diff-aware eval refresh across check, dry-run, targeted, and apply modes with manifest-snapshot rediscovery, per-primitive analyser refresh or CI cache reuse, framework-specific static stamping plus unified LLM regeneration, and strict preservation of user-authored cases and test files.

evals.expected_output: The answer enumerates per-primitive directories for skills, duplicated command and agent paths, hook bundle files, and cites hook:cursor-workspace as the canonical bundled hook target id.

evals.prompt: During a resumed apply flow the command hits a branch the skill docs do not cover. What must the skill return instead of calling AskQuestion, and which schema validates the payload?
assertions: When an undocumented palette-driven or resumed apply branch is reached, the skill returns structured `needs_user_input` validated against `plugins/zoto-eval-system/templates/schema/needs-user-input.schema.json`.
The skill does not call `askQuestion` from inside its implementation; interactive prompts remain command-owned.

primitive_analysis.summary: The skill governs diff-aware eval refresh across check, dry-run, targeted, and apply modes with manifest-snapshot rediscovery, per-primitive analyser refresh or CI cache reuse, framework-specific static stamping plus unified LLM regeneration, and strict preservation of user-authored cases and test files.

evals.expected_output: The assistant states the skill must emit structured needs_user_input for the command to present, must not call AskQuestion from the skill loop, and names the needs-user-input schema path.

evals.prompt: Someone edited `discoveryTargets` in live `.zoto/eval-system/config.yml` but we have not re-run create. Will a full-catalog `/z-eval-update --check` treat that edit as drift? Which discovery source applies for full rediscovery versus `--target` runs?
assertions: Full-catalog modes including `--check` and rediscovery without `--target` enumerate from `manifest.discovery_config`, not live `config.yml` discovery fields.
Targeted `--target` invocations use current `.zoto/eval-system/config.yml` discovery fields so narrowed scope matches the live scaffold.
Edits to live `config.yml` discovery fields alone do not alter full-catalog rediscovery until `/z-eval-create` refreshes the manifest snapshot.

primitive_analysis.summary: The skill governs diff-aware eval refresh across check, dry-run, targeted, and apply modes with manifest-snapshot rediscovery, per-primitive analyser refresh or CI cache reuse, framework-specific static stamping plus unified LLM regeneration, and strict preservation of user-authored cases and test files.

evals.expected_output: The reply clarifies full-catalog modes ignore live config discovery edits and read manifest.discovery_config, while targeted `--target` runs use live config.yml enumeration, and notes config-only edits do not change rediscovery until create refreshes the manifest snapshot.
